items.title: 'Towelling the bloke up in the cells': Shocking police emails revealed

children.content: Emails reveal an extraordinary exchange between AFP officers after an Indigenous man was allegedly beaten and illegally strip-searched at a Canberra watchhouse.

gallery.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

thumbnail.altText: A person's face and police tape and bars in a graphic image.

items.title: Fears $1m to remove lead-contaminated soil in Captains Flat 'won't go very far'

children.content: Residents of Captains Flat have welcomed the promise of a $1 million fund to help them remediate lead contamination on private properties, but some fear it will not be enough.

image.altText: Signs warning of dangerous lead contamination at the road to a closed mine.

items.title: Australian in Israeli Defence Force committed war crimes, report alleges

children.content: The Australian Federal Police is considering a brief of evidence from the Australian Centre for International Justice, detailing alleged war crimes in Gaza by an Australian citizen who was in the Israeli Defence Force.

image.altText: Badge on the sleeve of Australian Federal Police officer's uniform.

items.title: Canberra water catchment shows signs of climate change

children.content: Like melting glaciers in Antarctica or bleached coral on the Great Barrier Reef, Namadgi National Park is showing signs of damage linked to global warming.

gallery.altText: Views of a river in a national park

thumbnail.altText: Views of a river in a national park

items.title: Canberra man pleads guilty to performing Nazi salutes in public

children.content: A Canberra man has pleaded guilty after performing Nazi salutes at two suburban shopping centres and placing white supremacist stickers over Indigenous and multicultural signs at the Australian National University.

image.altText: A young man leaves court.

items.title: Jo Clay elected as leader of ACT Greens, Andrew Braddock named deputy

children.content: Jo Clay is elected leader of the ACT Greens in a party vote, with Andrew Braddock as deputy leader, ushering in a new era after Shane Rattenbury's 14 years at the helm.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair and a green blouse speaks, standing outside near a green hedge.

items.title: Comedian says 'terrifying' roadside assistance encounter has easy solution

children.content: Comedian Laura Johnston calls for changes to the law after saying she was confronted by an NRMA contractor who could not provide identification, behaved aggressively and swore at her before leaving her stranded in remote NSW.

gallery.altText: A woman with dark hair and glasses looking into the camera, speaking.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with dark hair and glasses looking into the camera, speaking.

avatar.alt: Niki Burnside is a journalist and digital producer based in Canberra.

items.title: When a fit young man disappears, he leaves behind a trail of strange clues

children.content: When the first major relationship of Jack McLennan's life implodes, he does what plenty of young men do: meets a mate for a quiet beer. But by morning, Jack's disappeared, leaving behind a series of mysterious clues and a devastated family.

gallery.altText: kellie stands in front of house holding sign saying "bring jack home"
the water at sunset at Ficks crossing
Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

thumbnail.altText: kellie stands in front of house holding sign saying "bring jack home"
the water at sunset at Ficks crossing
Jack McLennan and Carlee Beetham pictured happy together in front of a waterfall

items.title: Today Show ratings in 'freefall' as Channel Nine looks to replace Karl Stefanovic

children.content: The Today Show's ratings are in decline following the Nine Network's dumping of host Karl Stefanovic. Former head of News and Current Affairs at Seven, Nine, and 10, Peter Meakin says the audience will want to punish Nine, but things can turn around, even in breakfast TV.

image.altText: Karl Stefanovic in a suit, no tie smiling and standing in front of the Crown

items.title: Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon as Osaka stuns on and off court

children.content: Daria Kasatkina bows out of Wimbledon to end Australia's run in the women's draw.

image.altText: GettyImages-2283868659

items.title: Hugh Grant, Benson Boone among stars at rumoured Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wedding

children.content: A heavy media presence and barricades surround the Madison Square Garden stadium where fans wait in sweltering conditions.

items.title: Interpol issues wanted notice for Monaco bombing suspect

children.content: Investigators are hunting for a woman they say "disguised herself as a man" in connection with in a bomb attack that targeted a wealthy businessman in Monaco.

image.altText: Close up of a woman with dark hair, looking serious, wearing white t-shirt with blue stripes.

items.title: Indonesian soldiers recover body of US pilot killed in Papua

children.content: A video shows an operation by the Indonesian military to recover the body of an American pilot, shot dead by Papuan separatists in the Papua Highlands.

image.altText: Soldiers in uniform with guns put a dead body on a tarpaulin.

items.title: Rethinking drinking as gen Z wakes up to joy of early rising

children.content: Pub nights are becoming far less rowdy as young people swap big nights out for morning run clubs.

gallery.altText: Crowds outside of Romano's Pub on New Years Eve in the 90s

thumbnail.altText: Crowds outside of Romano's Pub on New Years Eve in the 90s

items.title: Boy left with broken eye socket after being hit by e-scooter

children.content: Cooper Blackshaw was not operating the e-scooter that injured him outside his school gates.

image.altText: A boy in a hospital bed with blood and a bandage on his eye

items.title: Ange Postecoglou appointed head coach at Saudi club Al-Nassr

children.content: The 60-year-old takes over the Riyadh-based side led by Portugal forward Cristiano Ronaldo.

image.altText: A man looks out onto a football field.

items.title: Pokémon GO was a 2016 phenomenon. Some people never stopped playing

children.content: One Pokémon GO fan walked 32,000 km in his search to "catch them all". On the way he found health, friendship and a partner.

items.title: ABC 5050 quiz: A July 4 special with an alien invasion, George III on TV and Taylor Swift

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

image.altText: Taylor Swift and Post Malone in the music video for her single Fortnight.

items.title: Why Avalon Emerson refuses to stay in one lane

children.content: Avalon Emerson has built a global reputation as a DJ, but she soon found standing still was not for her.

image.altText: Avalon Emerson lying in a grassy field in black dress and boots, head resting on arms, wildflowers around.

items.title: 175 years ago, Victoria struck gold. 230,000 scars remain

children.content: It has been 175 years since gold was first discovered in Victoria, and now a state government mapping project has discovered that there are far more relics of the gold rush left in the landscape than official records show.

a large hole behind a fence

items.title: Three men acquitted of murdering journalist Lyra McKee

children.content: The 29-year-old was shot by a member of a dissident Irish Republican Army splinter group while covering a riot in Derry/Londonderry in 2019.

image.altText: A young woman with short brown hair and glasses looks into the camera

items.title: 'Panicked' Putin has big decision to make, and 'could go nuclear'

children.content: The Russian president's decision to annex Crimea showed the world his authoritarian muscle. These days, it is a "personal humiliation".

image.altText: Vladimir Putin

items.title: Souths captain Cody Walker apologises after kicking opponent

children.content: South Sydney captain Cody Walker has said he let his teammates down after being sin-binned on Friday night for kicking an opposition player.

image.altText: Cody Walker of the Rabbitohs reacts angrily

items.title: Curnow hits front in Coleman Medal race as Swans and Crows record wins

children.content: Charlie Curnow kicks six goals as the Swans cruise to a 35-point win over the Bulldogs, while Adelaide holds off a fast-finishing West Coast in Perth.

image.altText: Two AFL players in red and white celebrate a goal together

children.content: While diners enjoy their seaside meals, Andrew Collins is happily outside chasing the scraps.

items.title: 'Similar to a blender': Gina Rinehart defends helipad amid noise concerns

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart wants to be able to land a helicopter on the roof of her corporate HQ, and says the noise experienced by nearby residents would be similar to using a coffee machine or blender.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart smiles as she is interviewed by a journalist.

items.title: China's latest activities near Taiwan indicate new strategy

children.content: Australia raises concerns about China's latest activities near Taiwan "directly to Chinese officials" as tensions simmer in the region.

image.altText: a Taiwan Coast Guard member monitor Chinese navy vessel

items.title: Speculation of 'reconciliation' as Prince Harry prepares to travel to UK

children.content: Prince Harry says he is still trying to bring his family to Britain next week, despite concerns of a "frenzy" as paparazzi attempt to snap rare photographs of his children.

items.title: MP calls for investigation into anti-abortion campaigner

children.content: Anti-abortion campaigner Joanna Howe has been called out for potentially breaching campaign financing laws, with one MP labelling her "attack" style as harmful and dangerous.

image.altText: Joanna Howe stands next to women holding up ban feticide sign.

items.title: NT shadow minister admits underage son drove car, considers resignation

children.content: Manuel Brown has admitted his underage son drove his personal car, one week after failing to disclose the matter when asked by the media, and says he is considering resigning from his shadow portfolios.

image.altText: an aboriginal man wearing a suit in parliament

items.title: Kyiv suffers deadliest attack this year, after Russia 'unleashed hell'

children.content: At least 27 people are dead and the city's key Red Cross warehouse has been destroyed, as search efforts are still underway.

image.altText: a burnt wheelchair covered in ash

items.title: Woman who attacked Senator Lidia Thorpe outside the MCG avoids jail

children.content: Senator Thorpe was with a group of friends near the stadium after the Dreamtime at the G game between Essendon and Richmond when she was attacked by Ebony Jan Bell.

image.altText: Ebony Bell leaves Magistrates Court in Melbourne

items.title: 'Grieving best mate' of West Coast Eagles star pens open letter to AFL

children.content: In a five-page letter to the executives and commissioners of the AFL, a grieving best mate has slammed the organisation for "failing" former West Coasts player Adam Hunter, who suffered from CTE.
